name: EnrollmentAgentOrchestrator_process_query_concurrent_throughput
description: Performance test that measures throughput and concurrency behavior of EnrollmentAgentOrchestrator.process_query under load. Uses stubbed dependencies (ChunkRetriever, LLMService, BusinessRulesEngine, TicketingAdapter, AuditLogger) that return quickly to simulate peak throughput. Runs N concurrent requests and asserts average latency and success.
input: Create EnrollmentAgentOrchestrator with injected stubs: ChunkRetriever.retrieve_chunks returns ['c1'], LLMService.call_llm returns LLMResponse(text='Summary\nActions: 1. Do X', tokens_used=None, raw_payload={}), BusinessRulesEngine.evaluate returns {'eligibility_status':'eligible','reasons':[]}, TicketingAdapter.create_ticket not invoked (eligibility eligible), AuditLogger.log_event returns a uuid. Launch N=50 concurrent asyncio tasks calling process_query(user_context={'student_id':'s'+i}) and measure total and per-request latency.
output: A list of envelopes (dict) returned for each concurrent invocation. Measured average latency and p95 latency must be within acceptable thresholds (example: avg < 300ms, p95 < 700ms) and all envelopes indicate success True.
success_criteria: no tasks raised exceptions in asyncio.gather
average per-request latency across N requests < 300 ms (configurable threshold)
error_scenarios: resource contention in test runner causes high latencies
one of the stubbed components raises unexpectedly causing some tasks to fail
asyncio scheduling starvation yields outlier latencies
